home.title: Welcome to Nirvana Biotech
home.subtitle: Leading the way with Translational research and innovation

about.title: About Our Scientific Mission
about.desc: Nirvana is a pioneering biotech firm dedicated to bridging the gap between human medicine and veterinary science. Through advanced molecular research, the company translates breakthrough discoveries from animal health applications into targeted, life-changing clinical therapies for humans.Core Focus AreasTranslational Molecular Research: Utilizing state-of-the-art genomics and molecular biology to understand complex diseases present across both human and animal populations.Veterinary Clinical Applications: Developing advanced therapeutics, diagnostics, and preventative care for companion and livestock health based on robust, cross-species molecular data.Human Therapeutics: Engineering next-generation treatments for chronic diseases, leveraging naturally sourced compounds and insights gained from comparative biology.The Nirvana AdvantageCross-Species Efficacy: By analyzing biological pathways conserved across species, the company rapidly identifies, validates, and de-risks treatments before taking them into human trials.Molecular Innovations: Moving beyond traditional pharmaceutical approaches to unlock the therapeutic potential of nature-derived molecules and advanced genetic applications.Holistic Health Pipeline: The research spans pain management, addiction mitigation, metabolic health, and immunology, benefiting both veterinary and human patients.

innovations.title: Dengue VLP development and validation
innovations.desc:
Construction: Designing the engineered plasmid with optimized signal sequences for HeLa cell expression.

Expression & Purification: Highlighting the transient expression and the successful isolation of spherical, ~39nm VLPs confirmed by TEM.

Immunization: Demonstrating the strong Dengue-1 specific IgG antibody response elicited in a murine model.

research.title: Dengue VLP development and production
research.desc:
The Goal: To develop a safer, more effective Dengue vaccine candidate using virus-like particles (VLPs)—structures that look like the real virus but cannot cause infection because they lack genetic material.

The Method: Researchers engineered a plasmid (genetic blueprint) containing Dengue-1 proteins, modified it with genetic signals from the Japanese Encephalitis virus to boost secretion, and grew it in human cells (HeLa cells).

The Results: The process successfully produced spherical VLPs that structurally mirror the native Dengue virus (about 39 nanometers wide). When tested in mice, these VLPs triggered a strong immune response, generating Dengue-1 specific antibodies.

The Takeaway: These HeLa-grown VLPs show strong potential for both new Dengue vaccines and diagnostic testing. The next step will be testing how effectively these antibodies actually protect against the live virus.
